explanation: The requirement states that the system should transition from Self-Test Mode to VentilationOff Mode (also referred to as StandbyMode in the APs) when either (1) the self-test procedure is completed successfully, or (2) when the GUI requests resuming ventilation. We model this as: whenever we are in SelfTestMode, if either 'selfTestPass' becomes true, or 'guiResumeRequest' becomes true, then in the next time step the system must be in StandbyMode (i.e., transition to VentilationOff Mode). This is captured by the template 'G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))', setting 'bool_exp2' to 'SelfTestMode & (selfTestPass | guiResumeRequest)' and 'bool_exp3' to 'StandbyMode'. This ensures that whenever in SelfTestMode and one of the trigger conditions occurs, the transition happens immediately at the next step.

The requirement states that the system should transition from SelfTestMode (self-test state) to VentilationOff Mode (StandbyMode in the atomic propositions) when either (1) the self-test procedure has succeeded, or (2) the GUI has requested to resume ventilation. The atomic propositions 'selfTestPass' (for successful self-test) and 'guiResumeRequest' (for GUI requesting ventilation resume) together cover the condition for triggering transition from SelfTestMode to StandbyMode. Therefore, the property should state: whenever the system is in SelfTestMode, and either selfTestPass or guiResumeRequest becomes true, then on the next step (i.e., after this, likely after the transition has been processed), the system should be in StandbyMode (VentilationOff). This temporal requirement is best captured by template 41, which states globally: whenever both SelfTestMode and (selfTestPass | guiResumeRequest) are true, then in the next step StandbyMode must be true.

This LTL property expresses that: always, when (1) Self-Test Mode is active and the self-test has passed, or (2) the GUI requests resumption of ventilation, then the system should eventually be (or immediately switch to) VentilationOff Mode (also known as Standby Mode). This matches the requirements: the transition from Self-Test Mode to VentilationOff Mode should occur when self-test completes successfully OR when the GUI asks for resuming ventilation. The antecedent combines the two conditions as a disjunction, and the consequent is being in StandbyMode. The globally operator enforces this at any time the antecedent holds.

The requirement specifies that the system should transition from Self-Test Mode to VentilationOff Mode when either: (1) the self-test procedure has been completed successfully, or (2) the GUI requests to resume ventilation. In terms of atomic propositions, 'SelfTestMode' corresponds to being in Self-Test Mode, 'StandbyMode' corresponds to being in VentilationOff Mode, 'selfTestPass' indicates successful completion of the self-test, and 'guiResumeRequest' indicates the GUI requesting to resume ventilation. This means, whenever in SelfTestMode and either selfTestPass or guiResumeRequest is true, the next state should be StandbyMode. This is best represented via the template: 'G((SelfTestMode & (selfTestPass | guiResumeRequest)) -> (X StandbyMode))', which at every point ensures that whenever SelfTestMode is active and either of the two conditions (selfTestPass or guiResumeRequest) occurs, VentilationOff Mode (StandbyMode) should be the next system state.

The requirement states that transition from SelfTestMode to VentilationOff (StandbyMode) should occur either when the self-test completes successfully (selfTestPass) or when the GUI specifically requests a ventilation resume (guiResumeRequest). Both events independently trigger the transition to VentilationOff Mode. Thus, the condition for leaving SelfTestMode transitioning to StandbyMode is the disjunction (OR) of selfTestPass and guiResumeRequest. LTL formula should specify that globally (at all times), if the system is in SelfTestMode and either condition occurs, then StandbyMode (VentilationOff) will hold in the next step. This matches a classic safety response property template.

The property requires that, globally, if the system is currently in SelfTestMode and either the self-test procedure passed (selfTestPass) or the GUI requested to resume ventilation (guiResumeRequest), then the transition to StandbyMode (which is described as VentilationOff Mode in the requirement) should happen next. This captures the requirement that the transition from Self-Test Mode to VentilationOff Mode (StandbyMode) occurs when either of the two specified events occurs.

The requirement specifies that the transition from Self-Test Mode to VentilationOff Mode (StandbyMode) must occur when either: (1) the self-test is complete and has passed (selfTestPass), or (2) the GUI issues a resume ventilation request (guiResumeRequest). To capture both cases, we require that whenever SelfTestMode is active and either selfTestPass or guiResumeRequest becomes true, then StandbyMode (VentilationOff) must follow (the transition should occur). This is an implication that for every step, if SelfTestMode and (selfTestPass or guiResumeRequest) both hold, then StandbyMode should become true next. The direct transition is best captured by the LTL template G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), where: bool_exp1 = SelfTestMode, bool_exp2 = (selfTestPass | guiResumeRequest), and bool_exp3 = StandbyMode. This ensures that as soon as the guard condition is met, in the next step, StandbyMode is entered as required by the specification.

The property requires that whenever the system is in SelfTestMode, a transition to VentilationOff Mode (StandbyMode) must occur as soon as either the self-test procedure completes successfully (selfTestPass) OR when the GUI asks for resuming ventilation (guiResumeRequest). This reflects the requirement that after SelfTestMode, entry to StandbyMode should only be triggered by either of the two events specified in the natural language: successful self-test or a resume ventilation request from the GUI.

To translate the specification that a transition from Self-Test Mode to VentilationOff Mode shall occur when (1) the self-test procedure has successfully been completed OR (2) the GUI asks for resuming ventilation, the relevant atomic propositions are 'SelfTestMode' (indicates Self-Test Mode is active), 'StandbyMode' (VentilationOff Mode), 'selfTestPass' (self-test completed successfully), and 'guiResumeRequest' (GUI asks for resuming ventilation). The property required is: if we are in SelfTestMode and either 'selfTestPass' or 'guiResumeRequest' becomes true, then in the next state (i.e., one step later), 'StandbyMode' (VentilationOff Mode) must be true. This is temporal (about a specific state change following an enabling event) and global (should hold at all times during execution). Template 41 fits: 'G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3))'. Here, 'bool_exp1' is SelfTestMode, 'bool_exp2' is (selfTestPass | guiResumeRequest), and 'bool_exp3' is StandbyMode. This template ensures that at any time, if the system is in SelfTestMode AND either the self test passes OR the GUI asks to resume ventilation, then in the next time step, StandbyMode is entered, exactly matching the contract.

The requirement states that the transition from Self-Test Mode to VentilationOff Mode (also called Standby Mode) shall occur when either the self-test procedure is completed successfully (selfTestPass) or when the GUI requests to resume ventilation (guiResumeRequest).

Therefore, the formalization must say: Whenever we are in SelfTestMode, and either selfTestPass or guiResumeRequest is true, then on the next step, StandbyMode (i.e., VentilationOff Mode) becomes true. This matches template 41, which expresses: 'Globally, if (SelfTestMode & (selfTestPass | guiResumeRequest)) then next StandbyMode.'

All the boolean expressions are well-formed: bool_exp2 = (SelfTestMode & (selfTestPass | guiResumeRequest)), bool_exp3 = StandbyMode.
